REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREGORY HAYNES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD AND DISCLOSE ITS ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF THE CASE. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER CLAIM AND ASSERTIONS MADE IN THAT VERIFIED COMPLAINT. SUCH ASSERTIONS ARE IN DIRECT CONTRADICTION TO THE CLAIMS IN THIS ACTION AND ARE NOT DISCLOSED IN THIS ACTION. WHILE PLAINTIFF CITES AUTHORITIES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF MAY PLEAD INCONSISTENT CLAIMS PREDICATED ON BOTH EXISTENCE AND ABSENCE OF AN AGREEMENT (HERE, LEASE), PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY THAT SUCH CLAIMS MAY BE ASSERTED IN DIFFERENT ACTIONS WITHOUT ANY DISCLOSURE OF PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF CERTAINTY AS TO THE FACTS IN EITHER CASE. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. AS PLAINTIFF ARGUES IN OPPOSITION DECLARATORY RELIEF IS AVAILABLE IN LIMITED JURISDICTION CASES PER CCP 580(B)(4) AS AUTHORIZED BY CCP 86. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW THIS CASE FALLS WITHIN ALLOWABLE CASES UNDER CCP 86(A)(7). TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT DEMURS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS BASED ON UNAVAILABILITY OF INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT DEMURRER IS A PROPER WAY TO ADDRESS ONE OF SEVERAL REQUESTS FOR RELIEF IN A CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT HOWEVER NOTES THAT WHILE PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT PERMANENT INJUNCTION IS NOT AVAILABLE IN LIMITED JURISDICTION CASES (OPPOSITION 9:14-15) PLAINTIFF NEVERTHELESS PRAYED FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION (COMPLAINT 7:14) AND DID NOT AMEND/WITHDRAW THIS PRAYER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREGORY HAYNES DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS AND EJECTMENT (FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION) IS OVERRULED. ALL ELEMENTS OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALLEGED AND THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION OF UNLAWFUL DETAINER (THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION) IS SUSTAINED. ANOTHER ACTION IS PENDING FOR THE SAME CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF DID NOT MOVE TO CONSOLIDATE THE ACTIONS AND DID NOT DISMISS THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION PRIOR TO AMENDING THIS COMPLAINT. ADDITIONALLY, THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS AS IT IS BASED ON A FATALLY DEFECTIVE NOTICE SEEKING RENT FOR MARCH TO AUGUST 2020. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A NOTICE OVERSTATING THE AMOUNT LEGALLY DUE MAY SUPPORT A COMPLAINT WHICH PURPORTS TO "STRIKE" THE OFFENDING OVERSTATED PORTION OF THE DEMAND. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Phil Foster, Esq., via Zoom, 415-626-7744 appearing for 6315 Third Street, Plaintiff.

Gregory Haynes, Defendant in Pro Per, in person.

Larry Haynes, Defendant in Pro Per, in person, 415-424-0283.

ARGUED. The Court questioned if parties have seen the court's tentative ruling. Both defendants responded yes. Mr. Gregory Haynes stated that he contested the tentative ruling and provided oral argument. Mr. Foster stated that the plaintiff is unaware of an oral lease, that the defendants have not paid rent, that plaintiffs are allowed to plead alternative and inconsistent theories and this amendment to the complaint is compliant with the prior court order. Mr. Gregory Haynes made an oral request to stay discovery. The Court denied that request. Mr. Gregory Haynes made an oral request to have 30 days to answer. Mr. Foster presented his objections. The Court denied that request.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GREGORY HAYNES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for trespass and ejectment (first and second causes of action) is overruled. All elements of these causes of action are alleged and the allegations are not uncertain. Demurrer to the causes of action of unlawful detainer (third and fourth causes of action) is sustained. Another action is pending for the same causes of action. Plaintiff did not move to consolidate the actions and did not dismiss the unlawful detainer action prior to amending this complaint. Additionally, the third cause of action fails as it is based on a fatally defective notice seeking rent for March to August 2020. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a Notice overstating the amount legally due may support a complaint which purports to "strike" the offending overstated portion of the demand.
Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)

Phil Foster; Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP; 415-626-7744; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff 6315 Third Street, LLC.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GREGORY HAYNES' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead and disclose its alternative theories of the case. The Court takes judicial notice of the unlawful detainer claim and assertions made in that verified complaint. Such assertions are in direct contradiction to the claims in this action and are not disclosed in this action. While Plaintiff cites authorities for the proposition that plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims predicated on both existence and absence of an agreement (here, lease), Plaintiff fails to cite any authority that such claims may be asserted in different actions without any disclosure of Plaintiff's lack of certainty as to the facts in either case. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the declaratory relief cause of action. As Plaintiff argues in opposition declaratory relief is available in limited jurisdiction cases per CCP 580(b)(4) as authorized by CCP 86. Plaintiff does not explain how this case falls within allowable cases under CCP 86(a)(7). To the extent defendant demurs to the cause of action for trespass based on unavailability of injunctive relief, demurrer is OVERRULED. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that demurrer is a proper way to address one of several requests for relief in a cause of action. The Court however notes that while Plaintiff admits that permanent injunction is not available in limited jurisdiction cases (Opposition 9:14-15) Plaintiff nevertheless prayed for permanent injunction (Complaint 7:14) and did not amend/withdraw this prayer. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
